source_claim: Garden and field routes contain pits, fallen fruit, fish, bees, crabs and other hazards requiring careful movement
Floodwater, birds, cattle, harvest labour, field songs, drums and ritual smoke define the Kāviri countryside
The travellers move slowly through inhabited agrarian country and halt repeatedly rather than crossing an empty landscape
Ascetic teachers appear at a river-side sacred place, teach on karma and liberation, and receive Kauṇṭi's declaration of faith
The couple and Kauṇṭi reach the southern bank and enter a flower grove where unfamiliar people can approach them
After hearing ascetic teaching, the travellers rest and resume their southward journey at dawn
Praise of the Pāṇḍya ruler links sovereignty, rain, agricultural abundance and southern territorial memory

zone_or_system: ROAD route knowledge and sacred geography
source_claim: A travelling Brahmin describes sacred sites, royal territory and the routes leading toward Madurai
Mullai and kuṟiñci landscapes become pālai under summer heat, showing ecology as seasonal transformation rather than fixed desert

zone_or_system: ROAD-Z04 arid route and cultivated hill
source_claim: One route passes dry trees, bamboo, thirsty deer and hunter territory before reaching crop-rich Sirumalai

zone_or_system: ROAD sacred cave and three ponds
anchor_phrase: புண்ணிய சரவணம் பவகா ரணியோடு இட்ட சித்தி
source_claim: A mountain-cave route is associated with three sacred ponds, guarded thresholds, mantras and promised knowledge or memory

zone_or_system: ROAD middle route and local deity
source_claim: The middle road crosses settlements and forests where a local deity may test travellers without physically harming them
Kauṇṭi rejects the cave quest and chooses truthful conduct, protection of life and direct travel toward Madurai
At a pond a forest deity assumes Vasantamālai's form, invokes Mādhavi and is exposed by Kōvalaṉ's protective mantra

zone_or_system: ROAD-Z05 heat avoidance and Aiyai shrine
source_claim: Because daytime heat makes the forest difficult, the group travels onward and reaches the Aiyai-Korravai shrine in hunter country
Kaṇṇaki's feet redden and the travellers rest in the shrine enclosure to recover from heat and road strain
Sālini becomes possessed and declares that the hunter community's decline follows an unpaid obligation to its goddess
The community dresses a maiden with serpent cord, tusk, tiger teeth, hide, bow, animals, colours, food, flowers, incense, drums, horn, flute and bell
In possession Sālini identifies Kaṇṇaki as an exceptional southern woman and future jewel of the world
Korravai is praised through weapons, animal skins, mountain, buffalo-demon and forms shared with wider divine traditions

zone_or_system: ROAD-Z06 night passage under Pāṇḍya order
source_claim: The travellers cross the dangerous summer region by moonlight, with the poem attributing unusual safety to just Pāṇḍya rule
They reach an outer settlement of learned religious householders and place Kaṇṇaki in protected lodging with Kauṇṭi
Kausika encounters Kōvalaṉ outside the city and recognises him through carefully framed speech about Mādhavi

zone_or_system: Pukār consequences carried along the road
source_claim: Kausika reports the grief of both merchant families, the search for Kōvalaṉ and Mādhavi's collapse and letter
The clay-sealed letter retains Mādhavi's scent; Kōvalaṉ understands her innocence, accepts his own fault and sends the message to his parents

zone_or_system: ROAD-Z06 music scent and first perception of Madurai
source_claim: Bards, yāḻ music, sandal, flowers, kitchens, sweet shops, household smoke, sacrifice and palace scents announce Madurai before it is seen
Temple and palace drums, Vedic recitation, ascetic teaching, elephants, horses and bardic dawn music reveal the capital's institutions
The Vaigai is personified through flowering banks, sand islands, fish-like eyes and concealed tears while sustaining the world around Madurai

zone_or_system: ROAD-Z06 Vaigai crossing and fortified edge
source_claim: The travellers cross by wooden raft, reach the south bank, circle moat and rampart, read ominous banners and enter cultivated outer precincts
